title: Fecal or caecal waters sample preparation for untargeted GCMS-based metabolomic
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bs1vf","text":"The following protocol describes the preparation of fecal or caecal water samples for non-targeted analysis by mass spectrometry coupled to gas chromatography as proposed by the PFEM (MetaboHUB-Clermont).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13i3q","text":"The steps cover the sample preparation and quality control. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9n0v3","text":"The expected outcome of this protocol is a list of fecal or caecal water samples ready for analysis by GCMS.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"b7u65","text":"Randomise the order in which samples are prepared and organize them according to this order.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9n47j","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Weigh samples as described in the table 1 and stored at -80 °C . Then, freeze dry or steal (or glass) ball agitator each sample. Species Quantity of stools milliQ water vol 1 Rat 100 mg 1 mL 2 mini Pig 300 mg 3 mL 3 Human 5 to 8 g not neccessary 4 Mouse 50 mg 0,5 mL Table 1 : stools quantity and milliQ water volumes depending on the species

Thaw FW or CW sample at 4 °C , vortex 00:00:10 then add a 2-fold volume of ice-cold ULC/MS grade methanol Biosolve Catalog #0013684101BS (CAS: 67-56-1) into FW or CW samples (Table2). Species FW or CW volumes 1 Mouse 300 µL 2 Rat 300 µL 3 Mini Pig 500 µL 4 Human 500 µL Table 2 : FW or CW volumes depending on the species

Dry samples completely using a EZ2.3 genevac system (Biopharma Technologies France). (Genevac EZ-2, EZ-2, https://www.spscientific.com/Products/Centrifugal_Evaporators___Sample_Concentrators/Genevac/EZ-2_Series/EZ-2_Series/, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/7mi2jk6.png )

Prepare a fresh solution of Methoxyamine hydrochloride Merck Catalog #89803-1G (CAS: 593-56-6) ( 15 mg/mL ) in Pyridine Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #360570 . The volume of this solution depends on the number of samples to be analysed ( 80 µL for each samples). Dissolve the dry samples in adding 80 µL of methoxylamine solution to each vial.
Add 2 µL of NaN3 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #S2002-5G (CAS: 26628-22-8) (an anti-microbial agent : 100 mg/mL ) per/g of FW or CW to the supernatants.

At the same time, a derivatization control sample (sample was substitued by milliQ water) was prepared in order to remove the background noise produced during sample preparation, derivatization, and GC/MS analysis.
Transfer 90 µL supernatant to glass vial. Spike 10 µL of 100 mg/mL IS L-VALINE (1-13C, 99%) Eurisotop Catalog #CLM-470-MPT-PK (CAS: 81201-85-6) ) to vial.
FW or CW aliquots depending to obtained volume and store at -80 °C in Eppendorf tubes to derivatization.
FW or CW come from homogenized stool samples (with or without water) ultra-centrifuged at 4 °C 50000 rpm, °C, (171500 g, Beckman Ti 70.1 rotor) 02:00:00 .
Fecal (FW) or caecal (CW) water samples were obtained from stools which are more or less dry depending on the species.
Vortex vigorously for 00:01:00 and incube at 37 °C for 24:00:00 (in order to inhibit the cyclization of reducing sugars and the decarboxylation of α-keto acids).
Add 80 µL of BSTFA + 1% TMCS Silylation Reagent Thermo Fisher Catalog #TS-38833 in the mixture and derivatize at 70 °C 01:00:00

Wait a few minutes for samples to cool and transfer 50 µL of derivatized mixture in a glass vial containing 100 µL of Heptane Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #246654-100mL (CAS: 142-82-5) prior to injection. Store blank and biological samples at 4 °C until further analysis
As well as a sample pool is formed from 10 µL of each extracted and derivatized sample to monitor the drift of the spectrometer during GCMS analysis.

Transfer 50 µL of sample pool in a glass vial containing 100 µL of heptane prior to injection, repeat this step 8 times (for preprocessing analysis). Store pool samples at 4 °C until further analysis.
Remove the supernatant and store it in Eppendorf tubes.
Vortex and keep the samples at -20 °C 00:30:00
Centrifuge the samples (Sigma 3-16PK, Fuscher Bioblock Scientific) 15000 rpm, 4 °C, 10 minutes
creator: Carole MIGNÉ
